sections.paragraph:
ἀμέριμνος, ον (s. prec.) free from care (so since the new comedy Philemon Com. 114; Menand., Fgm. 1083; Posidippus or Pla. Com. in Anth. Pal. 9, 359, 5; Comp. II 12ff; 137ff; also grave ins: Eranos 13, 1913, 87, no. 9, 5ff; pap; Wsd 6:15; 7:23; s. Nägeli 37, 1)

ⓐ of pers. θέλω ὑμᾶς ἀ. εἶναι I want you to be free from care (Appian, Maced. 19, §3 ἀμέριμνός εἰμι; Vett. Val. 355, 34 w. ἀλύπητος; Sext. Emp., Adv. Ethic. 11, 117 Syn. χωρὶς ταραχῆς) 1 Cor 7:32 (cp. Theophr. [Jerome, Adv. Jovin. 1, 47=Seneca, Fgm. 13, 47 Haase], who recommends celibacy because it makes one free for contemplation; PFay 117, 22 [108 a.d.] ἵνα ἀ. ᾖς; on the topic also cp. Epict. 3, 22, 69). ἀμέριμνον ποιεῖν τινα keep someone out of trouble (cp. PMich 211, 8 [c. 200 a.d.]; on Stoic ideas s. DBalch, JBL 102, ’83, 429–39; s. ἀπερισπάστως) Mt 28:14.

ⓑ of personal characteristics μακροθυμία Hm 5, 2, 3.—DELG s.v. μέριμνα. M-M.

prec. prec. = preceding

Philemon Com Philemon Com , IV–III b.c. (a few of the verses included here belong to Comp., q.v.)—List 5

Menand Menand , various dramas cited; IV–III b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Com. Com. = Comicorum Atticorum fragmenta (TKock) and related collections of comedy fragments as noted in text; various dates—List 5

Comp Comp date uncertain—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

Eranos Eranos , Acta Philologica Suecana—List 6

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

pers. pers. = person(s)

Appian Appian , I–II a.d.—List 5

Sext. Emp. Sext. Emp. = Sextus Empiricus, II–III a.d.—List 5

Syn.

Syn. = KAland, Synopsis Quattuor Evangeliorum—List 6

syn. = synopsis, synoptic

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Theophr Theophr , IV–III b.c.—List 5

PFay PFay = Fayûm Towns and Their Papyri—List 4

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

PMich PMich = Michigan Papyri—List 4

Stoic Stoic , various dates—List 5

JBL JBL = Journal of Biblical Literature—List 6

Hm Hm = Mandates

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 53
